summary: We recommend treating the D3 confusion matrix as the calibration baseline because scripts eval sh ran across all 20 passing and 20 failing fixture pairs and the python HeuristicVerifier perfectly classified each pair into TP=20 FN=0 TN=20 FP=0. The tradeoff against a more nuanced rubric is that a perfect score on the seed set means the verifier is currently a faithful gate but cannot distinguish between subtler shades of quality which D5 citation staleness and D6 URL liveness will need to address. The script exits non-zero when any false positive or false negative is observed which makes it a drop-in CI regression check for future rubric edits.

claims.statement: scripts eval sh runs the python HeuristicVerifier across both fixture buckets and emits evals results 2026-05-22 json containing totals confusion_matrix metrics and a 40-element pairs array because the shell script enumerates evals pairs passing and evals pairs failing then writes the structured result to disk.

citations.title: scripts eval sh source with the per-bucket scoring loop and the JSON emitter
evals results 2026-05-22 json captures totals matrix metrics and the per-pair verdict

claims.caveats: The script hardcodes the python interpreter at /Users/alexzh/...; the --python flag lets a CI runner override the path which is the documented production pattern.

claims.statement: the confusion matrix on the canonical floor of 3.5 records TP equal to 20 FN equal to 0 TN equal to 20 FP equal to 0 because every passing fixture scored at or above 3.5 and every failing fixture scored strictly below 3.5 with no parse errors.

citations.title: evals results 2026-05-22 json records the perfect classification matrix
passing MANIFEST md last verified 20 of 20 at or above 3.5

claims.caveats: Perfect classification on the seed set is expected because the failing pairs were hand-crafted to fire below the floor and the passing pairs are real merged PRs already gated; D4 will need to weigh whether the matrix is informative or merely tautological and may require additional naturalistic failing pairs to test the calibration.

claims.statement: scripts eval sh exits non-zero when any false positive or false negative is observed which means a future verifier change that flips a pair from the expected side of the floor will fail this script and thus fail any CI workflow that wraps it.

citations.title: scripts eval sh ends with an exit 1 conditional on FP or FN greater than zero
outcomes-mcp github workflows directory where a future eval-matrix job would live

claims.caveats: A CI workflow that wraps eval.sh is not part of D3 itself; D4 ADR will decide whether the matrix runs on every PR or only on rubric-touching PRs.

open_questions: Should D4 propose ratcheting the floor to 4.0 given that the current seed set has no pairs in the 3.5-4.0 band which would test the ratchet?
methodology_notes: Authored scripts eval sh with set minus euo pipefail bash and a per-bucket loop. Ran it against the 40 fixture pairs at floor 3.5 and the resulting JSON was inspected with python to confirm totals matrix and pairs are well-formed. Exit code 0 confirms zero FP and zero FN.
